target_lag_recursion.source_code_evidence: heavy_ruc_fullgrid_rescue_closure.evaluate_candidate seeds y_hist with actual log targets through origin and writes finite pred_log values into y_hist after each forecast horizon.

summary.policy_description: Source-code policy: seed y_hist with actuals through origin, then write each finite horizon prediction back before scoring later horizons.
summary.interpretation: Closest executable source-code replay. Remaining non-zero deltas, including horizon-1, indicate fitted-state or runtime drift beyond target-lag recursion choice.

summary.policy_description: Counterfactual policy: after each horizon, write the target period actual into y_hist.
summary.interpretation: Counterfactual recursion policy; larger deltas reject this as the parent implementation.

summary.policy_description: Counterfactual policy: seed y_hist with all actuals, including future validation periods.
summary.interpretation: Counterfactual recursion policy; larger deltas reject this as the parent implementation.

summary.policy_description: Counterfactual policy: after each horizon, write archived parent component prediction into y_hist.
summary.interpretation: Counterfactual recursion policy; larger deltas reject this as the parent implementation.

summary.policy_description: Counterfactual policy: seed y_hist with actuals through origin and never update during the forecast path.
summary.interpretation: Counterfactual recursion policy; larger deltas reject this as the parent implementation.

The source code uses recursive predicted target lags. Horizon-1 deltas remain non-zero, so the residual gap cannot be solved by lag recursion choice alone; parent fitted estimators or parent feature matrices are still missing.

Final weighted replay remains disabled unless all C1-C4 component replays and the weighted final replay pass.

governance_gap: Parent run did not retain fitted C1-C4 estimators or parent feature matrices. Source-refit state and feature matrices are exported, but C3/C4 and final weighted replay still exceed 1e-6 parity tolerance.
